title: Scotch Pancakes

overview.description: Light, fluffy Scotch pancakes with a tender crumb, perfect for breakfast or an afternoon snack.

ingredients: Butter or neutral oil for cooking
method: Mix together the flour, baking powder, sugar and salt.
Whisk together the egg, milk and melted butter.
Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients.
Whisk until just smooth. Don't overmix.
Leave the batter to rest for 10–15 minutes.
Heat a lightly buttered frying pan or griddle over a medium heat.
Drop approximately 2 tablespoons of batter per pancake.
Cook until bubbles appear across the surface and the edges begin to set.
Flip once and cook for another 1–2 minutes until golden.

learning.did-you-know: Scotch pancakes are thicker than crêpes but smaller than American pancakes, making them ideal for stacking or freezing.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How resting a batter and cooking over a moderate heat produces light, evenly risen pancakes.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A quick homemade treat using everyday cupboard ingredients that's ideal for breakfasts, lunchboxes or snacks.
chapman-tips: Resting the batter gives the baking powder time to start working and allows the flour to fully hydrate.
Cook over a medium heat—too hot and they'll burn before the middle cooks.
Wait until bubbles cover most of the surface before flipping.
The first pancake is usually the test one—adjust the heat if needed.
notes.batch-cooking: Cool completely before freezing between sheets of baking paper. Reheat in the toaster or microwave.
notes.leftovers: Excellent toasted the next day with butter and jam.
